model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"summaries","secondary_modes":["indexing","topk"],"rationale":"Isolate the schedule effect from the optimizer change by returning to the baseline Adam configuration while keeping the step-aligned cosine schedule. The last run worsened, so this is the cleanest low-complexity correction before trying larger architectural changes.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":["summaries"],"rationale":"The previous cosine-schedule runs degraded validation loss, so remove that source of instability and use a larger batch to expose each fixed step to more CIFAR-10 samples. Under a fixed-step evaluator, batch size is a direct way to raise effective data throughput without adding complexity.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The last gain came from increasing samples per step under the fixed-step budget. Doubling batch size again is the simplest way to push that effect further without adding architectural complexity or changing the optimizer dynamics.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"layout","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"The batch-size scaling is already paying off, which suggests the current network may now be the limiting factor. A modest depth and width increase is the simplest next capacity move that can still fit the compact CNN regime without changing the training loop.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 2","new":"DEPTH = 3","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["indexing"],"rationale":"The larger model and larger batch are already working, so the next low-complexity lever is regularization. A standard CIFAR-10 weight decay increase is a safe way to trim validation loss without changing the script structure.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":["topk","caching"],"rationale":"The last change nudged validation loss upward, so restore the weaker regularization and keep pushing the fixed-step data throughput. With this run still under a step budget, the next simplest lever is a larger batch so each update sees more of CIFAR-10.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["indexing"],"rationale":"The fixed-step run is now large-batch and capacity-rich, so the remaining lever is how aggressively Adam moves each step. A moderate learning-rate increase is the lowest-complexity way to test whether optimization, rather than model size, is now the bottleneck.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["indexing"],"rationale":"The previous learning-rate increase still improved validation loss, which suggests the current regime can absorb a bit more step size. With batch 512 and the larger CNN already in place, a slightly more aggressive Adam update is the cleanest next test.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["indexing"],"rationale":"The jump from 8e-4 to 1e-3 reduced performance, so the most plausible next move is to roll back to the better-tested learning rate. This preserves the larger batch and model capacity while avoiding further optimizer drift.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"layout","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The optimizer tuning is now near a plateau, while the model is still compact enough that a bit more width is a plausible source of gain. A moderate capacity increase keeps the successful large-batch training setup intact and tests whether the current bottleneck is representational rather than optimization-related.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["layout"],"rationale":"The widened model is now the main change, and the current learning rate may be a bit aggressive for final refinement. A small step-size reduction is the lowest-complexity way to test whether the larger network can settle to a better minimum without changing the training loop.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-4","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"indexing","secondary_modes":["caching","topk"],"rationale":"The plain Adam run with a reduced step size backslid, so the next coherent test is a decoupled-weight-decay optimizer while restoring the better step size. This keeps the successful large-batch, larger-model setup intact and probes whether optimizer dynamics are now the remaining bottleneck.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"summaries","secondary_modes":["topk","indexing"],"rationale":"The fixed-step run is now large-batch and relatively well-scaled, so the remaining clean lever is to anneal the learning rate over the budget instead of keeping it flat. I am also restoring the optimizer settings that previously gave the best loss before this branch drifted to a slightly worse AdamW variant.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"layout","secondary_modes":["summaries"],"rationale":"The cosine-schedule branch regressed sharply, so the safest move is to remove it and put the remaining budget into capacity, which has been the most consistently helpful lever in this run. The current model still looks small enough that a further width increase is the best chance to push validation loss below the current best.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 32","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["layout"],"rationale":"The widened model is now the main source of capacity, so a modestly stronger decay is the simplest way to test whether the run has shifted from underfitting toward a generalization-limited regime. This keeps the winning optimizer and batch setup intact while adding only one small regularization change.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["layout"],"rationale":"The larger model is already doing well, and the last improvement came from stronger regularization. Adding a small amount of dropout is the simplest remaining way to test whether the run is now overfitting slightly and can squeeze out a bit more validation gain.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.1","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"indexing","secondary_modes":["summaries","caching"],"rationale":"The Adam variants have effectively plateaued, and the dropout test was a clear regression. The remaining structural alternative is the classic SGD-plus-cosine setup for CIFAR-10, which is a better match for the current large-batch, high-capacity regime than another small Adam tweak.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"sgd\"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 0.05","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.1","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["indexing"],"rationale":"The cosine-scheduled SGD setup is the first configuration that clearly broke the Adam plateau, but the initial step size still looks conservative for a 512-batch CIFAR run. A higher starting learning rate is the simplest remaining knob to test while keeping the same optimizer and schedule shape.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 0.05","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 0.1","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["indexing"],"rationale":"The 0.1 step size was too aggressive, but the earlier 0.05 setting may still have been slightly conservative for this large-batch SGD run. A midpoint is the most reasonable final probe before giving up on the optimizer-tuning axis.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 0.1","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 0.075","text":"","source":""}]}
